summary: Add a CSV export affordance on the user list page. New API endpoint plus a UI button and download flow. Baseline treats data as internal (not GDPR-scoped) — the GDPR variant is a separate scenario.

reversibility.why: New additive surface; disabling the button and the endpoint rolls it back cleanly.

blast_radius.why: Touches UI and a new API route but doesn't change existing read paths or auth.

compliance_scope.why: Baseline assumption is internal data; would flip to MEDIUM/HIGH if PII export is in play (see scenario 10).

user_facing_impact.why: Users see a new button and a new download flow end-to-end.

novelty.why: CSV export on a list view is a familiar pattern; priors on existing list pages apply.

scope_effort.why: API endpoint + UI affordance + acceptance test coverage spans two teams' surface areas.

state_complexity.why: Read-only export; no new persistence or schema change.

operational_risk.why: Read path, no mutation; pagination strategy bounds memory pressure.

coordination_cost.why: Frontend + backend + QE need to align on contract and sync-vs-async export choice.

specialists.name: product-manager
specialists.why: Own scope and acceptance criteria for the export affordance.

specialists.name: solution-architect
specialists.why: Decide sync vs async export and pagination/limit strategy.

specialists.name: backend-engineer
specialists.why: Implement the CSV endpoint and streaming/pagination behavior.

specialists.name: frontend-engineer
specialists.why: Wire the button and the download flow into the user list page.

specialists.name: ui-reviewer
specialists.why: Polish visual affordance and verify download UX is coherent.

specialists.name: test-designer
specialists.why: Design the API, UI, and acceptance coverage; specifies evidence.

phases.name: clarify
phases.why: Confirm in-scope columns, sync/async choice, and row-count limits.

phases.name: design
phases.why: API contract and download flow designed together.

phases.name: test-strategy
phases.why: Plan the unit/integration/api/ui/acceptance coverage before implementation.

phases.name: build
phases.why: Backend endpoint and frontend button implemented in parallel once the contract is set.

phases.name: test
phases.why: Execute the planned pyramid and capture acceptance evidence.

phases.name: review
phases.why: Final UI review and senior sign-off before shipping.

rigor_why: User-visible and cross-surface, but all risk factors stop at MEDIUM — enforced gates are appropriate, full-rigor ceremony is not.
complexity_why: Mid-scale feature spanning API + UI with multi-role coordination but no migration or compliance amplifier.
open_questions: Sync download or async job + emailed link when row count is large?

tasks.title: Clarify export scope and row-count behavior
Design API contract and download flow
Plan test coverage (unit, integration, api, ui, acceptance)
Implement user-list Export button and download UX
End-to-end acceptance test covering happy path and pagination
